```{r setup-paths, include=FALSE}
# ─── Shared setup chunk, included by every pipeline Rmd via `child` ──────────
# DO NOT render this file directly. It is loaded at the top of every
# pipeline Rmd with:  child = "_setup.Rmd"

# ── 1. Project base directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# EDIT THIS ONE LINE on a new machine.  Point it to the folder that
# contains:   <base_dir>/Tsai CRC/2026 export/…
#             <base_dir>/Tsai CRC/Tsai sample list.xlsx
#             <base_dir>/Tsai CRC/CGMH_Biobank/GIMETB20171101_20221017_ES.xlsx
#             <base_dir>/output/  (will be created automatically)
#
if (!exists("base_dir")) {
  base_dir <- "/home/user/CGMH_Project"
}

paths <- list(
  base        = base_dir,
  data        = file.path(base_dir, "Tsai CRC", "2026 export"),
  sample      = file.path(base_dir, "Tsai CRC", "Tsai sample list.xlsx"),
  biobank_dir = file.path(base_dir, "Tsai CRC", "CGMH_Biobank"),
  results     = file.path(base_dir, "output", "results"),
  figures     = file.path(base_dir, "output", "figures"),
  tables      = file.path(base_dir, "output", "tables")
)

for (d in c(paths$results, paths$figures, paths$tables)) {
  if (!dir.exists(d)) dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE)
}
```

```{r setup-libs, include=FALSE}
# ── 2. User library path ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# On Windows, user-installed packages typically live under:
#   %USERPROFILE%\R\win-library\<R-version>
# On Linux/macOS, Sys.getenv("USERPROFILE") is empty and the default
# .libPaths() already points to the correct user library.
user_lib <- file.path(Sys.getenv("USERPROFILE"), "R", "win-library",
                      paste0(R.version$major, ".",
                             strsplit(R.version$minor, "\\.")[[1]][1]))
if (dir.exists(user_lib)) .libPaths(c(user_lib, .libPaths()))

# ── 3. Knitr defaults ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo       = TRUE,
  warning    = FALSE,
  message    = FALSE,
  fig.align  = "center",
  fig.width  = 8,
  fig.height = 6,
  dpi        = 150,
  cache      = TRUE,
  cache.lazy = FALSE,
  comment    = "#>"
)

# ── 4. Packages ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(readxl)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
  library(knitr)
})
```

```{r setup-helpers, include=FALSE}
# ── 5. Shared helper functions (previously in utils.R) ───────────────────────

#' Save a ggplot as both PDF and PNG in one call.
save_fig <- function(plot, basename, dir = paths$figures,
                     width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 200) {
  ggsave(file.path(dir, paste0(basename, ".pdf")),
         plot = plot, width = width, height = height)
  ggsave(file.path(dir, paste0(basename, ".png")),
         plot = plot, width = width, height = height, dpi = dpi)
  invisible(file.path(dir, basename))
}

#' Row-wise relative standard deviation (%) over a features × samples matrix.
#' Returns NA for rows with zero mean or all-NA values.
calc_rsd <- function(mat) {
  mu  <- rowMeans(mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  sig <- apply(mat, 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  rsd <- (sig / mu) * 100
  rsd[is.nan(rsd) | is.infinite(rsd)] <- NA
  rsd
}

#' Row-median imputation for a features × samples matrix (vectorised).
impute_row_median <- function(mat) {
  row_meds <- apply(mat, 1, median, na.rm = TRUE)
  na_idx   <- which(is.na(mat), arr.ind = TRUE)
  if (nrow(na_idx) > 0) mat[na_idx] <- row_meds[na_idx[, 1]]
  mat
}

#' D-ratio = SD(QC) / SD(biological) per feature. Values <0.5 indicate
#' good analytical precision relative to biological variation.
calc_dratio <- function(mat_qc, mat_bio) {
  sd_qc  <- apply(mat_qc,  1, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  sd_bio <- apply(mat_bio, 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  d      <- sd_qc / sd_bio
  d[is.nan(d) | is.infinite(d)] <- NA
  d
}
```
